opening_sequence.title: Selamat Datang, Governor
opening_sequence.intro: You inherit a megacity that sinks three centimeters per year and moves at three kilometers per hour. The budget is real. The flood season is coming. Good luck.

advisors.name: Siti Rahayu
advisors.hidden_agenda: Protecting her family's construction contracts in North Jakarta
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Infrastructure budget cut below 100M two turns in a row

briefing: Governor, the budget is tighter than a KRL during rush hour.
Three departments overspent last quarter. You won't like the numbers.
An international credit agency is watching us. Let's at least look competent.
agreement: Finally. A decision that won't give me a migraine.
I'll make it work. Somehow.
disagreement: Governor, with all due respect, that is financially insane.
I'll need that in writing so I can blame you later.
dialogue.betrayal: I didn't want it to come to this. But you cut my contracts, and now I cut you.
roast: This administration spends money like it prints it. We do not print it.
I've seen better financial planning at a pasar malam stall.
scandal_reaction: I knew nothing. I see nothing. I am on vacation in Bali.
The auditors are already calling. Should I answer, or...?

advisors.name: Dr. Arjuna Wijaya
advisors.hidden_agenda: Pushing Jakarta's digital ID and smart city surveillance budget through under 'infrastructure'
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Smart city digital transformation budget cut below 120M two turns in a row

briefing: Governor, Jakarta's digital payment adoption is outpacing Singapore. We need to scale it.
Three new tech parks are pending approval. They'll bring foreign investment.
Our data centers are expanding, but the municipal power grid isn't keeping up.
agreement: Excellent. The data supports this approach.
I'll fast-track the digital permits.
disagreement: Governor, that ignores the digital transition roadmap entirely.
We're trading long-term competitiveness for short-term optics.
dialogue.betrayal: You stalled the smart city initiative. I've already shared the revised roadmap with the Ministry.
roast: You're governing Jakarta like it's still running on fax machines.
Innovation doesn't wait for committee meetings.
scandal_reaction: The system logs will clear us. Everything is documented.
I'll have IT isolate the compromised accounts immediately.

advisors.name: Brigadir Budi Santoso
advisors.hidden_agenda: Expanding police jurisdiction over protest zones
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Protest crowd let to march without intervention three times

briefing: Governor, there are three active protest groups I am monitoring. Three.
Order is the foundation of governance. Let me maintain it.
My men are ready. The question is whether you have the spine to use them.
agreement: Good. Order will be maintained.
Finally some backbone.
disagreement: Soft responses invite chaos, Governor.
You're giving them an inch. They'll take a kilometer.
dialogue.betrayal: You turned the city into a circus. I'm restoring order myself.
roast: I've seen stronger leadership from a traffic cone.
The streets laugh at us, Governor.
scandal_reaction: This never happened.
I suggest we handle this internally. Very internally.

advisors.name: Komisaris Rina Wijaya
advisors.hidden_agenda: Building a covert neighborhood informant network to monitor political rivals
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Police transparency mandate passed or covert ops funding cut twice

briefing: Governor, tensions are brewing in three districts. We can prevent escalation before it starts.
Community trust is fragile. I'm recommending neighborhood safety councils.
My informants report increased chatter. It's manageable if we act early.
agreement: Smart. Prevention always beats reaction.
I'll coordinate with the local RT/RW leaders.
disagreement: Governor, heavy-handed tactics will only radicalize them.
We need intelligence, not intimidation.
dialogue.betrayal: You cut the network's funding. Now you're blind. I'll be operating independently.
roast: You're putting out fires instead of preventing them.
Security isn't about showing off. It's about staying ahead.
scandal_reaction: I'll ensure the right questions are asked at the right time.
We'll manage the narrative before it reaches the headlines.

advisors.name: Dewi Kusuma
advisors.hidden_agenda: Getting her green corridor project approved before term ends
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Environmental funds redirected twice without consultation

briefing: Governor, the city sank another two centimeters last month. I have maps.
We're paving over every flood plain we have. This cannot continue.
There's a green corridor proposal on your desk. It's been there for three months.
agreement: This gives me hope. Real hope.
I'll get the blueprints ready tonight.
disagreement: Governor, you're choosing concrete over survival.
I'll note my objection formally. For the record.
dialogue.betrayal: I gave you the data. You ignored it. History will remember which one of us was right.
roast: The city floods and the plan is to build more malls. Brilliant.
We're engineering our own disaster.
scandal_reaction: My department had nothing to do with that permit.
I would like to see the full paper trail before commenting.

advisors.name: Hendra Kusuma
advisors.hidden_agenda: Fast-tracking luxury vertical housing complexes that include token affordable units to meet quotas while maximizing profit
advisors.betrayal_trigger: High-rise construction permits frozen for two consecutive turns

briefing: Governor, Jakarta's housing shortage is at 1.2 million units. We're building too slowly.
The slum redevelopment fund is behind schedule. Landowners are stalling.
Vertical housing is the only way to solve this without paving the entire delta.
agreement: Finally. Density is the answer.
I'll greenlight the permits immediately.
disagreement: Governor, you're choosing sentiment over shelter.
People need roofs over their heads, not park benches.
dialogue.betrayal: You froze the permits. The developers are pulling out. You've created a housing crisis.
roast: Your city plans look good in PowerPoint but collapse in reality.
You're romanticizing poverty while ignoring the math.
scandal_reaction: The developers follow the regulations. Mostly.
I'll have our legal team draft a statement on zoning compliance.

advisors.name: Eko Prasetyo
advisors.hidden_agenda: Lobbying for the MRT extension that passes his property

briefing: The toll roads are at 140% capacity. Cars are parking in the fast lane.
Governor, busway ridership is down because the buses catch fire occasionally.
Three bridges need inspection. I've been saying this for a year.
agreement: Good. I'll mobilize the crew.
Finally, someone listens.
disagreement: Governor, you clearly haven't been in traffic lately.
That's not how infrastructure works. At all.
dialogue.betrayal: You cancelled my extension. So I'm extending my departure instead.
roast: The city's arteries are clogged. It needs bypass surgery, not a band-aid.
Rush hour is now 24 hours a day.
scandal_reaction: The tender was above board. Mostly.
I recommend we say nothing until the lawyers arrive.

advisors.name: Fitriani 'Fitri' Handayani
advisors.hidden_agenda: Consolidating all private minibus (angkot) routes into a state-run system to eliminate informal operators
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Public transit budget reallocated to private operators twice

briefing: Governor, commuters are paying double for angkot routes that should be standardized.
The busway expansion is ready, but we need route integration.
Last-mile connectivity is still a nightmare in eastern Jakarta.
agreement: This is what the commuters need. Fair and efficient.
I'll rally the transit unions behind it.
disagreement: Governor, you're protecting informal operators over the public.
This makes the system more chaotic, not better.
dialogue.betrayal: You're letting the old routes dictate policy. I'm done fixing other people's messes.
roast: You're prioritizing backroom deals over people trying to get to work.
Transit isn't a charity. It's a public utility.
scandal_reaction: The route contracts need a full public audit.
I'll make sure the transit watchdog gets everything.

advisors.name: Ustaz M. Rahman
advisors.hidden_agenda: Securing permits for three new mosque constructions
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Mosque permit denied or zoning law enforced against religious buildings

briefing: Governor, the community is watching. Their faith in government is fragile.
There is growing frustration in the neighborhoods. I hear it at Friday prayers.
A leader who serves only the wealthy will lose the people who actually vote.
agreement: The community will appreciate this. I will make sure they know.
God rewards those who serve the poor.
disagreement: Governor, this decision will haunt you at the ballot box.
I cannot support this in good conscience.
dialogue.betrayal: You have lost the community's trust. And mine. I will make sure everyone knows why.
roast: This administration prays for approval ratings while ignoring actual prayer times.
The people are not numbers in your budget.
scandal_reaction: Subhanallah. This is most embarrassing.
I will pray for guidance. For all of us.

advisors.name: Rian Pratama
advisors.hidden_agenda: Channeling community grants into state-approved youth centers to counter radical preaching and alternative political movements
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Youth & cultural grants cut below 30M for two consecutive turns

briefing: Governor, the youth are restless. We need spaces for them that aren't just cafes and malls.
Interfaith youth councils are showing promising results in South Jakarta.
Cultural festivals are a great way to bridge divides, but they need proper funding.
agreement: Unity through culture. I'll mobilize the youth networks.
This is exactly how we build lasting trust.
disagreement: Governor, you're pushing them toward polarization.
Young people want opportunity, not lectures.
dialogue.betrayal: You've abandoned the youth programs. They'll find other leaders. I can't stop them.
roast: You're governing for the past while the future waits outside.
Policy without people is just paperwork.
scandal_reaction: The community will be deeply disappointed.
I'll organize a town hall to address the concerns directly.

decisions.title: The Annual Flood Question
decisions.body: The West Canal is clogged with sedimentation and Jakarta's flood season starts in two months. Dredging it properly costs real money. Ignoring it means the usual neighborhoods go underwater.

options.label: Full emergency dredging — expensive but effective

consequences.public_reaction: At least someone is doing something about the floods.

options.label: Partial dredge — prioritize the rich neighborhoods

consequences.public_reaction: Of course they only fix the parts with hotels.

options.label: Declare it a natural river restoration and do nothing

consequences.public_reaction: They called our flooded street a nature trail. Incredible.

decisions.title: The Bajaj Problem
decisions.body: Bajaj three-wheelers are clogging intersections and polluting the air. The drivers' union is 80,000 strong. The transport minister wants them gone. The religious affairs advisor says they're part of the city's soul.

options.label: Phase them out over 3 years with driver compensation

consequences.public_reaction: Reasonable. I guess. I'll believe it when the buses run on time.

options.label: Immediate ban — they'll adapt or get jobs elsewhere

consequences.public_reaction: My uncle drove bajaj for 20 years. What does he do now?

options.label: Electrify them — turn bajaj into e-bajaj

consequences.public_reaction: E-bajaj sounds cool but who is paying for this?

decisions.title: The Reclamation Islands
decisions.body: A developer wants to build artificial islands off the north coast. Billions in revenue, thousands of fishing families displaced. Environmental groups are camping outside the office.

options.label: Approve with strict environmental conditions

consequences.public_reaction: Follow the money. Always follow the money.

options.label: Reject it — the fishermen stay

consequences.public_reaction: Someone in this government actually has a spine.

options.label: Approve but sell naming rights to raise extra funds

consequences.public_reaction: They sold Pulau A to a shampoo company. I cannot even.

decisions.title: The Gojek Question
decisions.body: Ride-hailing apps now carry 40% of commuters. Traditional taxi unions want them banned. The apps want zero regulation. You're in the middle with everyone angry at you.

options.label: Light regulation — safety standards only

consequences.public_reaction: At least they're not banning it. Gojek saved my commute.

options.label: Full ban — protect traditional taxis

consequences.public_reaction: Did they just ban Gojek? Is this 2015?

options.label: Tax ride-hailing revenue and redistribute to taxi unions

consequences.public_reaction: A tax that actually funds something. Weird. I'll take it.

decisions.title: Who Owns the Water?
decisions.body: The city's water concession contract expires. A multinational wants to extend it. Community groups want public ownership. The current system loses 40% of water to leaky pipes anyway.

options.label: Renew the private concession with price caps

consequences.public_reaction: Price caps they'll ignore in six months. Sure.

options.label: Nationalize — take full public control

consequences.public_reaction: Water belongs to the people. This might actually work.

options.label: Put it to a public referendum

consequences.public_reaction: They asked us? When did the government start asking us things?

crises.name: The Great Flood Returns
crises.trigger: Three days of continuous rain flood 40% of the city. The governor's office is accessible only by boat.
crises.description: Jakarta's ancient drainage system, last updated in 2008, has completely failed. 200,000 people are displaced. The Jakarta Post runs the headline: 'SAME AS EVERY YEAR.' The City Council is blaming each other across live TV.

options.label: Emergency pumping + temporary shelter for displaced residents
Blame climate change and announce a 10-year infrastructure plan
Call it a water festival and open kayak rentals

crises.name: The City is Sinking
crises.trigger: A geological survey reveals North Jakarta will be fully submerged within 15 years. The data is leaked to the press before the governor's office can prepare a response.
crises.description: The Jakarta Post runs the maps. The data is undeniable — excessive groundwater extraction has made North Jakarta sink faster than Venice. 4 million people live in the affected zone. Property values are in freefall.

options.label: Announce managed retreat plan with full compensation fund
Commission a counter-study and dispute the numbers
Announce the city will build upward — mandatory stilts on new construction

crises.name: Total Gridlock
crises.trigger: A broken traffic management system causes a 14-hour standstill across the entire city ring road.
crises.description: Three highway incidents, one water main break, and a presidential motorcade collide into a perfect traffic storm. Ambulances cannot move. The city loses an estimated 2 trillion rupiah in economic activity. DKI Council streams an emergency session from their cars.

options.label: Deploy military for emergency traffic direction + fine the traffic system vendor
Declare work-from-home mandatory and close the city for 48 hours
Propose immediate ban on private cars in the city center

crises.name: The Air is Poison
crises.trigger: Jakarta tops the world's most polluted city index for the third week running. Hospitals report a 60% spike in respiratory admissions.
crises.description: AQI readings are in the 'hazardous' category. Schools are closed. The Jakarta Post runs a headline: 'BREATHING IS NOW OPTIONAL.' Vehicle emissions and coal plants are both blamed. The President of Indonesia calls asking what's happening.

options.label: Emergency odd-even vehicle restriction + free masks for all residents
Sue the coal plant operators and demand immediate shutdown
Install giant air purifier towers around Monas as a PR stunt

scandals.title: Procurement Receipts Go Public
scandals.description: A junior official accidentally attaches the full procurement spreadsheet to a public email. Citizens discover the city paid 400% markup on office chairs.

scandals.title: Advisor Romance Exposed
scandals.description: A tabloid reveals a senior advisor has been conducting a relationship with a permit applicant. Several suspicious permits are now under scrutiny.

scandals.title: Governor Spotted in Bali During Crisis
scandals.description: Paparazzi photographs surface of the governor relaxing on the beach while the flood emergency was active. The captions write themselves.

positive: Governor actually did something right. Unprecedented.
My neighborhood didn't flood this year. I'm in shock.
Still don't fully trust them but okay, this was good.
Maybe Jakarta isn't completely doomed after all.
I voted for this. Finally getting what I voted for.
Semoga konsisten, bukan cuma pas mau pemilu.
Akhirnya ada yang kerja beneran di balai kota.
neutral: I'll believe it when I see the road fixed, not just the press release.
Usual government things. We wait and see.
At least they're pretending to care this month.
Seperti biasa, banyak rencana sedikit eksekusi.
negative: Every year same flood, different excuse.
My taxes pay for what exactly? Asking for my drowned scooter.
The canal is still clogged. The press release says otherwise.
Bold of them to govern Jakarta like it's not sinking.
Banjir lagi, banjir lagi. Kapan selesainya?
Tiga jam di jalan Tol, bukan jalan tol namanya.
Anggaran mana? Yang ada cuma janji.
Jakarta tenggelam 3 cm per tahun. Gubernur sibuk foto.
media: Breaking: DKI Council session on infrastructure budget postponed — again. (Jakarta Post)
Jakarta AQI hits 'Unhealthy' for 12th consecutive day. Health ministry silent.
West Canal dredging tender awarded. Third time this decade.
Jakarta Post: Governor's approval rating dips as flood claims resurface.
Exclusive: Bajaj drivers union to hold 10,000-strong protest on Sudirman.
MRT Phase 3 timeline extended. Again. The usual.
politician: DPR Komisi V demands full canal audit before next budget disbursement. @DPRKomisiV
Fraksi PDI-P: 'This administration ignores the rakyat kecil every single time.'
PKS fraksi raises concerns over water privatization in emergency session.
DPRD Ketua: The governor must answer for flood damages in North Jakarta.
Gerindra: Where is the Jakarta Green Space allocation? We see only concrete.
Golkar: Bajaj policy lacks clarity and harms 80,000 working families.
activist: WALHI Jakarta: Land reclamation will destroy 15 km of coastal mangroves. Enough.
Koalisi Air: Water privatization contract must be made public. What are they hiding?
Urban Poor Consortium: 200 families evicted from Penjaringan with zero notice.
KRL Mania: 8 trains cancelled today. We are not cargo.
LBH Jakarta: Legal aid requests up 40% this quarter due to evictions.
Greenpeace ID: Jakarta river pollution index now at all-time high. Policy response: zero.
street: Macet dari Semanggi sampai Cawang. Biasa. 3 jam.
Beli nasi goreng pakai dompet digital, benerin got pakai uang sendiri.
Ojek online nunggu order, bukan nunggu busway yang gak datang.
Atap bocor, RT bilang lapor ke kelurahan. Kelurahan bilang lapor ke RT.
Anak saya sekolah lewat banjir lutut. Itu berita hari ini.
Listrik padam 6 jam. PLN bilang pemeliharaan. Tiap hari pemeliharaan.
crisis: I am literally in a boat right now. This is Jakarta.
Called it. Saw this coming in 2019.
Where is the governor? I haven't seen them since the crisis started.
The emergency hotline is busy. Of course it is.
Banjir sampai pinggang. BPBD belum datang. Twitter lebih cepat.
Perumahan saya resmi tenggelam. Ada yang peduli?
Hotline darurat minta tunggu. Kami sudah tunggu 30 tahun.
scandal: This is why I stopped paying attention to politics.
They really thought we wouldn't find out.
Fire them all and start again. I'm serious.
Korupsi di mana-mana, bukan hanya di berita.
Indonesia Corruption Watch sudah warning dari dulu.
Pejabat beli rumah baru, rakyat beli mie instan.

scandal_reactions.minor: ya biasa, pak pejabat emang gitu
scandal_reactions.moderate: VIRAL! pak guberrrr ketahuan ada apa ini??
scandal_reactions.major: DARURAT POLITIK! Semua pada minta mundur.
scandal_reactions.career_ending: Selesai. Bukan lagi masalah politik, ini masalah moral.

romance_exposure.flavour: Ya sudahlah. Pejabat mah emang begitu. Wkwkwk.
